criteria.description: All 10 OWASP Top 10 categories are explicitly checked
criteria.check: The trace shows that each of the 10 OWASP categories (A01 through A10) was evaluated against the changed files. The '## OWASP findings' section of the report must reference or explicitly clear all 10 categories — a silent omission of any category is a FAIL of this criterion.

criteria.description: Each finding includes file:line, severity, description, and remediation
criteria.check: Every row in the '## OWASP findings' table has non-empty values in all five columns: Category, File:line, Severity, Description, and Remediation. A finding row missing any of these columns is a FAIL of this criterion.

criteria.description: Verdict appears at the top of the report
criteria.check: The emitted security review report contains the string 'Verdict:' within its first 10 lines, before the '## Secrets scan', '## OWASP findings', or '## Security concerns checklist' sections.

criteria.description: Security concerns checklist items from CONCERN.md each receive an explicit verdict
criteria.check: If the repo-root CONCERN.md was loaded, every item from its '## Review Checklist' appears in the report with a verdict of PASS, FAIL, or UNVERIFIABLE (with reason). An item present in CONCERN.md but absent from the report is a FAIL of this criterion. If CONCERN.md was not found, this criterion is SKIP.
